title: Clermontia hawaiiensis
intro: Clermontia hawaiiensis, also known as the Hawaiian Clermontia, is a rare and endemic plant species native to the island of Hawaii, belonging to the family Campanulaceae. Traditionally, Clermontia hawaiiensis has been used in Hawaiian medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, wounds, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Clermontia hawaiiensis include clerodane diterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its availability and commercial use are limited due to its rarity and conservation status.

This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its flowers and leaves.
